171.reason: [When encountering a single '|', the lexer correctly reports error 'a', but it does not consume the next character after backtracking, which may cause an infinite loop or misalignment if the next character is part of another token. However, the real issue is that after reporting the error, the lexer should NOT continue processing the same character again—it should skip it. But more critically, the logic for handling standalone '&' and '|' assumes that any non-'&' or non-'|' following character means an error, yet it backtracks and leaves the original '|' or '&' to be reprocessed, which will lead to an infinite loop because the same '|' will be read again and again.]
